text: Hope you guys are ready to celebrate.
We've got to give Highguard a big round
of applause for their latest
achievement. For the first time ever
since release, the concurrent player
count actually dropped below 3,000 as
they reached a new all-time low of 2,729
concurrent players on Steam. But hey,
that's fine. The boss man himself, Chad
Grineer of Highgard, said that it
doesn't matter how many people play the
game. What matters most is that the game
is loved by the people who play it.
Well, that sure is nice, Chad. But, you
know, one one little problem here. If
the player count's low enough, the
servers are going to be offline and then
no one can love the game. So, yeah, the
player count kind of matters. You kind
of got to make money if you want to keep
the servers online, I would imagine.
Crazy concept. 7 hours ago. Even the
most diehard fans of Highguard over on
the official Highguard subreddit are
beginning to feel the impending doom for
this one, saying, "As much as I wanted
to love the game, this game might not
survive February. This actually becoming
a top post there with 188 up votes and
saying, "It takes so much time to get a
lobby now." The top post here in the
comments reading, "Let's see what this
next patch brings, but if it doesn't
bring in enough new players or people
back, I think it might be dead by
March." The second top comment saying,
"The writing was on the wall, but anyone
who raised concerns was told that they
were just a part of the hate bandwagon.
How the Echo Chamber serves only to
self-sabotage a fan base." And the third
top upvoted comment here reading, "I
genuinely enjoyed the game. Played for
about 35 hours. The characters and
gameplay are good, but am I supposed to
just endlessly grind a casual mode in a
very sweaty competitive game? There's no
progression, no stats, no ranked, no
variety of modes, no custom lobbies, no
training maps, no replays, no skin
markets, no drop in/dropout deathmatch.
If I feel like a chill or less effort
session, this game doesn't offer that,
so I'm just going to play something else
instead. Wait, that's actually crazy.
They wanted this to be a competitive
game and they don't even tell you any
stats. What were they cooking with this?
Another top comment bringing up how
there's no progression, no rewards, no
leveling, nothing like that. Yeah, this
this is actually a pretty shocking state
of affairs for this game. It's crazy the
team ever thought this was suitable to
release. And taking one final look at
the player count here. When 5v5 mode was
released around January 31st, the player
count did shoot back up to 15K. Well,
okay, shoot back up is a little bit of
an overstatement, but they had a they
had a little spike back to 15K.
Unfortunately for them, they couldn't
hold those levels. Nowhere close to it.
And yeah, here we are. That does it for
my high guard update. There's obviously
not too much to talk about here. Uh
yeah, it doesn't look good. Let me know
what you think in the comments.
